mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 4.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and accelerating consumer prices creates an overheating demand environment that forces monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive GDP and CPI growth yields positive equity returns in more than 60 percent of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production indicates weakening economic activity that reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with industrial production changes with a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains above 3.0 percent, further increases in the federal funds rate successfully anchor expectations and stabilize markets, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns do not materialize when the federal funds rate rises alongside consumer price index growth exceeding 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rate indicates robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The employment-unemployment divergence fails to predict positive returns with an out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in retail sales signals immediate consumer demand contraction that reduces quarterly earnings forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines more than 2 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of months following a retail sales drop exceeding 2 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing GDP growth during a supply-constrained regime indicates capacity utilization bottlenecks that pressure margins and yield negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production outperformance relative to GDP correlates with positive equity returns in more than 55 percent of observations.
